claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 6 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: do you contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep? This could include payments for things like food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards his accommodation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more important question to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (for example, the person he lives with)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure if anyone else is claiming for him or not.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all the information you've provided. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, let me explain the situation:

To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to be responsible for the child. You're usually considered responsible if either:
- You live with the child, OR
- You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes, or pocket money

Since Alex doesn't live with you and you're not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep, you would not meet the responsibility criteria for Child Benefit.

The person Alex lives with would typically be the one eligible to claim Child Benefit for him, as they would be the one responsible for his day-to-day care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

However, if your circumstances change in the future - for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if you start making regular financial contributions towards his upkeep that are at least equal to the Child Benefit amount - you may become eligible at that point and could make a claim then.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16) and the claimant lives in the UK, which are positive factors. However, to qualify for Child Benefit, the claimant must be responsible for the child. Under the rules, you're usually responsible if you either live with the child OR pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). The claimant does not live with Alex and is not contributing financially towards his upkeep. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the responsibility requirement and is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has a 6-year-old son, Alex, who does not live with them. The claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. As they do not meet the responsibility criteria (neither living with the child nor contributing financially), they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

As they do not meet the responsibility criteria (neither living with the child nor contributing financially), they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
